title: Monosis kannikattiensis
taxonomy: Monosis kannikattiensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Monotropaceae. It is a mycoheterotroph, meaning it obtains its nutrients by parasitizing fungi associated with tree roots. Monosis kannikattiensis is native to India and is considered to be an endangered species. The classification of this plant is still being researched and studied by botanists. Further classification details are needed to fully understand its taxonomic position.

Monosis kannikattiensis is an endangered, epiphytic orchid native to the Western Ghats region of India, belonging to the family Monotropoideae. The health benefits of Monosis kannikattiensis include its potential use in treating various diseases, such as fever, respiratory disorders, and skin conditions, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Monosis kannikattiensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
